lines.text: Bay Two closes today. [pause] Every one of you is paid twelve months at full rate, and your medical stays on the whole time.
There's a card coming to you. [pause] It puts you first in line if this floor ever runs again.
(then) I don't expect you to believe that means anything. [pause] Keep the card anyway.
[angry] My father cut the first nozzle ring in this bay!
I know he did. [pause] His name is on the wall of the visitor center.
(then) The tooling gets declared surplus and sold at public auction. An outside outfit comes in to pallet it up and count it.
[quietly] Whatever you need to think about today, put it on me.
[hesitates] The Monitorship views this as a, a meaningful posture of alignment. I'll be noting the, ah, expedited timeline favorably.
[flatly] I just got fired, Mr. Boone.
I know what happened. [pause] Keep your tools.
